page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 96 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for integer and tile operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with the server achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core execution, and ray tracing. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth are also in the top 25% of tested systems. The hardware demonstrates strong throughput for static web serving and database operations. However, single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average tier. For machine learning workloads, prompt processing for small and medium models is highly efficient, though text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom performance tier.

From a resource-density perspective, the n4-highcpu-48 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not demand a large memory footprint. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory data processing. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage options. This server is optimized for multi-threaded compute tasks, web serving, physics simulations, cryptography, and small-to-medium scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs across 24 physical cores and 96 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on network storage and CPU-based execution. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, particularly in Passmark CPU tests, ray tracing, and large-block memory bandwidth. It also delivers high throughput for static web serving and database operations, though single-core metrics and large-model LLM text generation are average to weak. With a low 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-dense, multi-threaded workloads such as physics simulations, cryptography, and medium-scale model prompt processing, while presenting a clear tradeoff for memory-heavy applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and no local storage. It delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for compute-dense workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 features 48 Intel vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-dense, multi-threaded server featuring advanced matrix extensions and high memory bandwidth for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong multi-core performance in Passmark, ray tracing, and database operations
High uncached memory bandwidth for large-block read and write tasks
No local storage or GPU resources, requiring network-attached storage
Optimized for multi-threaded compute, cryptography, and web serving
